message.content:
READ-ONLY documentation-drift sizing audit for heat ₣BB in the Recipe Bottle project at /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster. Do NOT edit any file. Compare each assigned veiled spec against the current code it describes, and report drift.

Specs live in `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/`. Code lives in `Tools/rbk/`.

BB landed these themes (VERIFY against actual code — do not trust this list blindly):
- JWT-SA access probe: propagation-absorbing bounded retry — first 2xx wins, HTTP 403 retries within a max_attempts × delay_ms budget, all other codes (including 401) fall to a single fail-fast catch-all with a body-summary diagnostic. This REPLACES the old "all iterations must pass; 403→fatal; 401→fatal" semantic. Call-site: rbte_engine.sh::rbte_probe defaults shifted to 60 × 3000ms (≈180s) for SA roles only; Payor OAuth probe defaults/semantic unchanged.
- IAM grant/propagation contracts may have shifted with the depot-identity-collapse and SA cult-verb work.

YOUR ASSIGNED SPECS:
- RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe.adoc
- RBSAO-access_oauth_probe.adoc
- RBSAX-access_setup.adoc
- RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc
- RBSCIP-IamPropagation.adoc
- RBSGS-GettingStarted.adoc

RELEVANT CODE to compare against: rbgv_AccessProbe.sh, rbte_engine.sh, rbte_cli.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, rbgo_OAuth.sh, rba_Auth.sh (all under Tools/rbk/). Read what you need; grep for the functions the specs name.

For EACH assigned spec return ONE ledger row:
- Spec acronym + filename
- Verdict: `in-sync` | `drifted-minor` | `drifted-major` | `stale-orphan` (describes a renamed/removed operation)
- Note: one line — what specifically shifted, or "matches code"
- Magnitude: S (few lines) / M (a section) / L (substantial rewrite) / — (in-sync)

Be skeptical: a spec being recently edited often means it is already synced. Distinguish already-synced from genuinely drifted. Return ONLY the ledger rows in a markdown table, no preamble.

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

19	# Recipe Bottle Google Verification - JWT SA and Payor OAuth access verification
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBGV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBGV_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbgv_*)
29	
30	zrbgv_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBGV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgv already kindled"
32	
33	  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
34	  zrbgc_sentinel
35	  zrbgo_sentinel
36	  zrbuh_sentinel
37	  zrbgp_sentinel
38	
39	  # Kindle-constant temp file paths for forensic visibility
40	  readonly ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_resp.json"
41	  readonly ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_code.txt"
42	  readonly ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_stderr.txt"
43	  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_resp.json"
44	  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_code.txt"
45	  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_stderr.txt"
46	
47	  # Transient-5xx retry policy: 3 attempts with initial 2s backoff doubling each retry (worst-case +6s)
48	  readonly ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
49	  readonly ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
50	
51	  readonly ZRBGV_KINDLED=1
52	}
53	
54	zrbgv_sentinel() {
55	  test "${ZRBGV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgv not kindled - call zrbgv_kindle first"
56	}
57	
58	# Convert milliseconds to a decimal seconds string suitable for sleep
59	zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture() {
60	  zrbgv_sentinel
61	
62	  local z_ms="${1}"
63	
64	  buc_log_args "Converting ${z_ms}ms to sleep duration"
65	
66	  # Compute whole seconds and remainder milliseconds using integer arithmetic
67	  local z_sec=$(( z_ms / 1000 ))
68	  local z_rem=$(( z_ms % 1000 ))
69	
70	  # Format as decimal with three fractional digits (sleep accepts e.g. 1.500)
71	  printf '%d.%03d' "${z_sec}" "${z_rem}"
72	}
73	
74	# Resolve the RBRA file path for a given role name
75	# Role names: governor | director | retriever
76	zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture() {
77	  zrbgv_sentinel
78	
79	  local z_role="${1}"
80	
81	  case "${z_role}" in
82	    governor)
83	      test -n "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
84	      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}"
85	      ;;
86	    director)
87	      test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
88	      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
89	      ;;
90	    retriever)
91	      test -n "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE is not set"
92	      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
93	      ;;
94	    *)
95	      buc_die "Unknown role '${z_role}': expected governor | director | retriever"
96	      ;;
97	  esac
98	}
99	
100	# HTTP GET with bounded exponential-backoff retry on transient 5xx responses.
101	# On any non-5xx response (including auth errors), populates z_code_file and returns.
102	# On curl-network failure or exhausted 5xx retries, buc_die. The caller evaluates
103	# the final HTTP status via its own case-switch on the populated z_code_file.
104	zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry() {
105	  zrbgv_sentinel
106	
107	  local -r z_label="${1}"
108	  local -r z_url="${2}"
109	  local -r z_token="${3}"
110	  local -r z_resp_file="${4}"
111	  local -r z_code_file="${5}"
112	  local -r z_stderr_file="${6}"
113	
114	  local z_attempt=1
115	  local z_delay="${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
116	  local z_curl_status=0
117	  local z_code=""
118	
119	  while test "${z_attempt}" -le "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; do
120	    buc_log_args "${z_label}: HTTP GET attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"
121	
122	    z_curl_status=0
123	    rbuh_request "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}"          \
124	                      "${z_resp_file}" "${z_code_file}" "${z_stderr_file}" \
125	      || z_curl_status=$?
126	
127	    buc_log_args "${z_label}: curl exit status ${z_curl_status}"
128	    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"
129	
130	    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 \
131	      || buc_die "${z_label}: curl failed (network/SSL/DNS) — see ${z_stderr_file}"
132	
133	    z_code=$(<"${z_code_file}") || buc_die "${z_label}: failed to read HTTP code file"
134	    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "${z_label}: empty HTTP code from curl"
135	
136	    case "${z_code}" in
137	      500|502|503|504)
138	        if test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; then
139	          buc_step "${z_label}: transient HTTP ${z_code}, retrying in ${z_delay}s (attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
140	          sleep "${z_delay}"
141	          z_delay=$(( z_delay * 2 ))
142	          z_attempt=$(( z_attempt + 1 ))
143	        else
144	          buc_die "${z_label}: repeated transient HTTP ${z_code} after ${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts — see ${z_resp_file}"
145	        fi
146	        ;;
147	      *)
148	        return 0
149	        ;;
150	    esac
151	  done
152	}
153	
154	# Extract '.error.message' from a JSON response file for diagnostic surfacing.
155	# Returns a fallback string for empty/missing/malformed bodies. jq stderr captured
156	# to the paired stderr file (no suppression). Exactly one line printed in all paths.
157	zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture() {
158	  zrbgv_sentinel
159	
160	  local -r z_resp_file="${1}"
161	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2}"
162	
163	  test -s "${z_resp_file}" \
164	    || { printf 'empty response body\n'; return 0; }
165	
166	  jq -e . "${z_resp_file}" > /dev/null 2>>"${z_stderr_file}" \
167	    || { printf 'non-JSON response body — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"; return 0; }
168	
169	  jq -r '.error.message // "no error.message field"' "${z_resp_file}" 2>>"${z_stderr_file}" \
170	    || printf 'jq extraction failed — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"

182	  local -r z_attempt="${2}"

229	zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once() {
230	  zrbgv_sentinel
231	
232	  local z_iteration="${1}"
233	
234	  buc_log_args "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}"
235	
236	  buc_log_args "Authenticate via Payor OAuth refresh token flow"
237	  local z_token

239	    || buc_die "Failed to obtain Payor OAuth access token (iteration ${z_iteration})"

262	
263	  case "${z_code}" in
264	    200)

266	      ;;
267	    401|403)

269	      ;;
270	    *)
271	      local z_err=""
272	      if jq -e . "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
273	        z_err=$(jq -r '.error.message // "Unknown error"' "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null) || z_err="Unknown error"

282	######################################################################
283	# External Functions (rbgv_*)
284	
285	# Probe 1: JWT SA Access Probe
286	#
287	# Bounded propagation-absorbing retry. First HTTP 200/206 wins; HTTP 403 (the
288	# IAM-policy-read 'not yet' signature in the post-mint window) retries with delay;
289	# any other HTTP code dies with body summary. Past max attempts on 403, dies with
290	# body summary as 'budget exhausted'. Layers cleanly with rbgo_get_token_capture's
291	# JWT-signature retry: each layer recognizes its own 'not yet' and retries.
292	#
293	# All three roles have read access:
294	#   Governor  = Owner
295	#   Director  = repoAdmin
296	#   Retriever = reader
297	rbgv_jwt_sa_probe() {
298	  zrbgv_sentinel
299	
300	  local -r z_role="${1:-}"
301	  local -r z_count="${2:-1}"
302	  local -r z_delay_ms="${3:-0}"
303	
304	  buc_doc_brief "Probe JWT service account access for a given role against Artifact Registry"
305	  buc_doc_param "role"     "Role to probe: governor | director | retriever"
306	  buc_doc_param "count"    "Maximum attempts; first 2xx wins, retries on HTTP 403 (default: 1)"
307	  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between retries (default: 0)"
308	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
309	
310	  test -n "${z_role}"  || buc_die "role parameter required (governor | director | retriever)"
311	  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"
312	
313	  buc_step "JWT SA access probe: role=${z_role} count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"
314	
315	  local z_iter=1
316	  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
317	    buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}/${z_count}"
318	
319	    local z_code
320	    z_code=$(zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once_capture "${z_role}" "${z_iter}") \
321	      || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: probe-once observation failed"
322	    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: empty HTTP code"
323	
324	    case "${z_code}" in
325	      200|206)
326	        buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
327	        return 0
328	        ;;
329	      403)
330	        if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}"; then
331	          buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: 'not yet' (HTTP 403); retrying"
332	          if test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
333	            local z_sleep
334	            z_sleep=$(zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture "${z_delay_ms}")
335	            buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next attempt"
336	            sleep "${z_sleep}"
337	          fi
338	        else
339	          local z_err
340	          z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
341	          buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: propagation budget exhausted (HTTP 403): ${z_err}"
342	        fi
343	        ;;
344	      *)
345	        local z_err
346	        z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
347	        buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
348	        ;;
349	    esac
350	
351	    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
352	  done
353	
354	  buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}]: loop exited without verdict — internal logic error"
355	}
356	
357	# Probe 2: Payor OAuth Access Probe
358	#
359	# For each iteration:
360	#   1. Authenticate via zrbgp_authenticate_capture (RBRO refresh token -> access token)
361	#   2. Call CRM projects.get on payor project to verify token works
362	#   3. Sleep for configured delay
363	rbgv_payor_oauth_probe() {
364	  zrbgv_sentinel
365	
366	  local z_count="${1:-1}"
367	  local z_delay_ms="${2:-0}"
368	
369	  buc_doc_brief "Probe Payor OAuth access against Cloud Resource Manager (CRM) projects.get"
370	  buc_doc_param "count"    "Number of iterations (default: 1)"
371	  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between iterations (default: 0)"
372	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
373	
374	  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"
375	
376	  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe: count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"
377	
378	  local z_iter=1
379	  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
380	    buc_step "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iter}/${z_count}"
381	
382	    zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once "${z_iter}"
383	
384	    if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}" && test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
385	      local z_sleep
386	      z_sleep=$(zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture "${z_delay_ms}")
387	      buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next iteration"
388	      sleep "${z_sleep}"
389	    fi
390	
391	    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
392	  done

394	  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe complete: iterations=${z_count}"
395	}

1	[[rbscig_iam_grant_contracts]]
2	==== IAM Grant API Contracts
3	
4	Research memo documenting the actual behavioral contracts of the Google Cloud
5	IAM policy APIs used by the four grant patterns:
6	{rbtoe_iam_grant_project}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo},
7	{rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}.
8	Also covers Secret Manager ({rbtoe_iam_grant_secret}).
9	
10	This document provides verified evidence for spec definition corrections and
11	future convergence decisions.  It should be re-reviewed when any grant function
12	behavior is modified, when new resource types require IAM grants, or when
13	Google API behavior changes are suspected.
14	
15	Findings are verified against API reference documentation, upstream proto
16	definitions (`google.iam.v1.Policy`, per-service `service.proto` files), and
17	ecosystem evidence (Terraform provider issues, Red Hat bugzilla, Pulumi issues).
18	
19	Research conducted: 2026-03-11.
20	
21	===== HTTP Method and Body Format
22	
23	[cols="2,2,2,2",options="header"]
24	|===
25	|API
26	|getIamPolicy
27	|setIamPolicy
28	|SET body format
29	
30	|CRM Projects ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project})
31	|POST (body: `{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}`)
32	|POST
33	|`{"policy":...}`
34	
35	|IAM {giam_service_account_s} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_sa})
36	|POST (body: `{}`)
37	|POST
38	|`{"policy":...}`
39	
40	|{gar_registry} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_repo})
41	|GET (query param)
42	|POST
43	|`{"policy":...}`
44	
45	|Secret Manager ({rbtoe_iam_grant_secret})
46	|GET (query param)
47	|POST
48	|`{"policy":...}`
49	
50	|{gcs_service} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket})
51	|GET (query param)
52	|PUT
53	|raw policy (no envelope)
54	|===
55	
56	The GET-vs-POST split reflects an API generation boundary.
57	{gcs_service} predates Google's gRPC transcoding convention (AIP-127/AIP-136)
58	and uses classical REST semantics (GET to read, PUT to replace).
59	{gar_registry} and Secret Manager define their own RPC methods in their
60	service protos with GET HTTP annotations, overriding the default IAM mixin POST.
61	CRM Projects and IAM {giam_service_account_s} use the standard IAM mixin POST
62	convention.
63	
64	Note on gRPC transcoding tolerance: the {gar_registry} proto specifies GET
65	for `getIamPolicy` with no `additional_bindings` for POST.  AIP-127 documents
66	no automatic POST-to-GET fallback — if the proto annotation says `get:`, only
67	GET is accepted via the transcoded REST endpoint.  The previous implementation
68	sent POST to this GET-only endpoint, which worked intermittently via undocumented
69	transcoding tolerance but was observed failing with HTTP 404 on 2026-03-12
70	during director creation (the 404 was the transcoding layer rejecting the POST,
71	not a missing repository).  The inline director AR grant path also passed an
72	empty body `{}` that omitted `requestedPolicyVersion`, creating a latent
73	condition-mangling hazard.  Both issues are now corrected: the shared
74	{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo} function already used GET; the inline director path
75	has been fixed to match.
76	
77	===== Etag Behavior
78	
79	[cols="2,2,2,2",options="header"]
80	|===
81	|API
82	|Etag in GET response
83	|Mismatch error
84	|No-etag behavior
85	
86	|CRM Projects ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project})
87	|Always present
88	|409 ABORTED
89	|Unconditional overwrite
90	
91	|IAM {giam_service_account_s} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_sa})
92	|Always present
93	|409 ABORTED
94	|Unconditional overwrite
95	
96	|{gar_registry} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_repo})
97	|Always present
98	|409 ABORTED
99	|Unconditional overwrite
100	
101	|Secret Manager
102	|Always present
103	|409 ABORTED
104	|Unconditional overwrite
105	
106	|{gcs_service} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket})
107	|Always present
108	|412 Precondition Failed
109	|Unconditional overwrite
110	|===
111	
112	**Universal finding: every API always returns an etag.**
113	The `etag` field is a required structural field of the `google.iam.v1.Policy`
114	proto message (`bytes etag = 3`).  There is no documented scenario where a
115	successful `getIamPolicy` response omits the etag.
116	
117	**Unconditional overwrite is dangerous.**
118	All five APIs accept writes without etag and silently succeed.  If the existing
119	policy is version 3 (IAM Conditions) and the caller sends a version 1 policy
120	without etag, all conditions are silently destroyed.  The proto documentation
121	explicitly warns: "If you omit the etag field, then IAM lets you overwrite a
122	version 3 allow policy with a version 1 allow policy, and all of the conditions
123	in the version 3 policy are lost."
124	
125	**{gcs_service} is the only 412 API.**
126	All standard IAM mixin APIs (CRM, IAM, {gar_registry}, Secret Manager) return
127	409 ABORTED on etag mismatch.  {gcs_service} returns 412 Precondition Failed.
128	This is a genuine API family difference, not arbitrary — {gcs_service} uses
129	metageneration-based preconditions and `If-Match` header semantics from its
130	legacy REST design.
131	
132	**The single-writer invariant is necessary but not sufficient.**
133	The operational invariant at the top of `rbgi_IAM.sh` ("Single admin actor.
134	There are no concurrent writers in the same project.") protects against
135	concurrent _Recipe Bottle_ operations.  However, Google's own internal
136	processes — API enablement auto-grants, service agent auto-provisioning,
137	organization policy inheritance changes — can modify project IAM concurrently
138	and without notice.  This is why {rbtoe_iam_grant_project}'s etag handling is
139	load-bearing even under single-writer, and why {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo} and
140	{rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} should also use etag intentionally rather than relying on
141	the single-writer assumption alone.
142	
143	**Accidental etag passthrough in current code.**
144	The compose function (`rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture`) applies
145	`if $etag != "" then .etag = $etag else . end` — when the caller passes empty
146	string for etag, the jq filter does nothing, leaving the original `.etag` field
147	from the `getIamPolicy` response in the composed JSON.  This means
148	{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo} and {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} may already be _accidentally_
149	sending etags via passthrough to `setIamPolicy`.  Whether those APIs then
150	enforce the passthrough etag is unverified.  The current behavior is neither
151	intentionally safe (explicit etag extraction and assertion) nor intentionally
152	unsafe (deliberate unconditional overwrite) — it is accidental, and should be
153	made intentional in either direction.
154	
155	===== Policy Version and requestedPolicyVersion
156	
157	The `requestedPolicyVersion` parameter in `getIamPolicy` controls which policy
158	schema the caller declares it understands:
159	
160	[cols="2,2,3",options="header"]
161	|===
162	|requestedPolicyVersion
163	|Policy has conditions?
164	|Result
165	
166	|Omitted or 1
167	|No
168	|Returns version 1 policy
169	
170	|Omitted or 1
171	|Yes
172	|Returns version 1; conditions appear as mangled role names with `_withcond_` suffix + hash (e.g., `roles/iam.securityReviewer_withcond_58e135cabb940ad9346c`). Actual condition objects are stripped.
173	
174	|3
175	|No
176	|Returns version 1 policy (server won't inflate version unnecessarily)
177	
178	|3
179	|Yes
180	|Returns version 3 policy with full condition objects
181	|===
182	
183	Currently only {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} requests version 3.
184	{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, and {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}
185	do not request a specific version.  If any of those resources ever acquire
186	conditioned IAM policies, the `getIamPolicy` response would silently mangle
187	the conditions, and a subsequent `setIamPolicy` would destroy them.  This is
188	a latent hazard: Recipe Bottle does not currently use IAM Conditions on these
189	resource types, but future work or organization-level policy inheritance could
190	introduce them without Recipe Bottle's knowledge.
191	
192	===== updateMask Default Behavior
193	
194	The `setIamPolicy` request includes an optional `updateMask` field (FieldMask).
195	When not provided, it defaults to `bindings, etag`.  This means the `version`
196	and `auditConfigs` fields in the submitted policy JSON are _ignored_ by the
197	server unless the mask explicitly includes them.  The compose function should
198	not need to manage `auditConfigs` (Recipe Bottle does not configure audit
199	logging via IAM policy), but this default explains why submitting a policy with
200	version=1 does not downgrade a version=3 policy when the mask is defaulted —
201	the version field is simply ignored.
202	
203	===== Empty-Policy / No-Policy Behavior
204	
205	[cols="2,3,1",options="header"]
206	|===
207	|API
208	|getIamPolicy on new resource
209	|404 possible?
210	
211	|CRM Projects ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project})
212	|200 + policy with bindings (project born with Owner grant)
213	|No (403 if project does not exist — deliberate enumeration prevention)
214	
215	|IAM {giam_service_account_s} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_sa})
216	|200 + empty policy with etag
217	|No
218	
219	|{gar_registry} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_repo})
220	|200 + empty policy with etag
221	|No
222	
223	|Secret Manager
224	|200 + empty policy with etag
225	|No (404 = secret itself does not exist)
226	
227	|{gcs_service} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket})
228	|200 + policy with inherited bindings
229	|No
230	|===
231	
232	**No API returns 404 for "no IAM policy."**
233	All resources that accept allow policies are created with default allow policies.
234	`getIamPolicy` returns 200 with an empty-ish policy (no bindings, but etag and
235	version present) for resources that have never had explicit IAM configuration.
236	
237	===== Propagation Error Catalog
238	
239	Three propagation classes feed into `zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate`'s
240	per-site tolerance list (see <<rbscip_trade_study>> for the shared backoff
241	profile):
242	
243	[cols="1,1,2,2,2",options="header"]
244	|===
245	|Class
246	|HTTP code
247	|Body glob
248	|Scope applicability
249	|Discriminator
250	
251	|A — forward member-visibility
252	|400
253	|`*does not exist*`
254	|All grant sites (project + resource scope)
255	|Body match; message content stable across ecosystem
256

258	|400
259	|`*is not deleted*`

261	|Body match; message content stable across ecosystem
262	
263	|C — caller-recently-empowered
264	|403

266	|Resource-scope sites only ({rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_secret}, plus the inline GAR retry in {rbtgo_director_invest})

269	
270	The HTTP 400 errors containing `"does not exist"` and `"is not deleted"` that
271	the grant functions retry on are **empirically stable but not contractually
272	guaranteed**.  Google does not document error message text as stable API
273	surface.  The HTTP status code (400) is the stable signal; the message
274	content is diagnostic, not machine-parseable contract.
275	
276	The two 400 patterns represent different propagation directions:
277	
278	* `"does not exist"` — forward propagation: a newly-created {giam_service_account}
279	  is not yet visible to the IAM policy service as a valid member.  The SA
280	  resource has been created (GET returns 200) but the policy service has not yet
281	  learned about it.
282	
283	* `"is not deleted"` — backward propagation: a recently-deleted
284	  {giam_service_account} is still referenced in existing policy bindings.
285	  The SA resource has been deleted but the policy service still considers it
286	  a valid (or at least referenced) member.
287	
288	Multiple independent ecosystem sources (Terraform provider, Red Hat OpenShift
289	installer, Pulumi) have depended on these strings for years without breakage.
290	The Terraform Google provider explicitly treats HTTP 400 as retryable in IAM
291	contexts (issue #8280), which constitutes strong ecosystem evidence that this
292	pattern is reliable enough for major infrastructure tools.
293	The practical risk of string instability is low but nonzero.
294	
295	The HTTP 403 class C pattern represents **caller-empowerment propagation**
296	into resource-scope IAM caches: the caller (governor) has been freshly
297	granted `roles/owner` on the depot project, but the artifact-registry
298	resource-scope IAM cache has not yet learned about the grant, even though
299	project-scope and SA-scope caches already have (the asymmetry observed
300	2026-05-13 against `artifactregistry.repositories.getIamPolicy` on a
301	fresh-depot AR repo, with `roles/owner` granted ~30 seconds prior).
302	Cumulative additional propagation observed: ~30 seconds.
303	
304	Class C uses **time-bound discrimination** rather than body matching: real
305	propagation succeeds within the `RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC` budget;
306	real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly.  This avoids the trap of
307	treating "denied because forbidden" as "denied because lagging" — the
308	distinction is observational, not parseable from the 403 response.  The
309	same time-bound discriminator is already proven in
310	{rbtgo_access_jwt_probe}'s `invalid_grant` retry harness.
311	
312	Class C tolerance is **scope-discriminated**: project-scope grant sites
313	({rbtoe_iam_grant_project}) do not declare 403 tolerance because the
314	governor's `roles/owner` reaches the project-scope cache effectively
315	instantly (no observed lag).  Adding spurious 403 tolerance at project
316	scope would slow down real-denial diagnosis without absorbing any known
317	race.
318	
319	**SA email vs numeric ID propagation.**
320	Google's documentation recommends using a service account's numeric ID rather
321	than its email address to reference it immediately after creation: "To identify
322	a service account just after it is created, use its numeric ID rather than its
323	email address."  Recipe Bottle uses email addresses throughout, which is why
324	propagation retry is necessary.  Switching to numeric ID could reduce (but
325	likely not eliminate) propagation delays.
326	
327	**SA keys.create write-path propagation.**
328	Observed 2026-03-12 during fresh depot onboarding: `serviceAccounts.keys.create`
329	POST returned HTTP 404 "Service account ... does not exist" immediately after
330	both `serviceAccounts.get` (GET by email) and `serviceAccounts.keys.list` (GET)
331	succeeded on the same SA email path.  This demonstrates a third propagation
332	class: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) propagate independently
333	from SA write operations (keys.create) on the same endpoint family.
334	The 404 response confirms the POST was rejected without side effects — no key
335	was created — making retry safe.  `gcloud iam service-accounts keys list`
336	confirmed zero USER_MANAGED keys after the 404 failure.
337	The race is intermittent: a subsequent director creation (dir1011) with the same
338	code path succeeded on first attempt.
339	Mitigation: retry keys.create POST on 404 up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX`
340	times with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts.
341	
342	===== Secret Manager Regional Endpoints
343	
344	Secret Manager supports both global and regional secret endpoints:
345	
346	* Global: `projects/*/secrets/*`
347	* Regional: `projects/*/locations/*/secrets/*`
348	
349	The service proto defines `additional_bindings` for regional secrets at both
350	`getIamPolicy` and `setIamPolicy`.  The current {rbtoe_iam_grant_secret} grants use global endpoints.  If Recipe Bottle ever uses regional
351	secrets, the IAM grant patterns would need to construct regional resource paths.
352	Noting for completeness.
353

437	===== Convergence Analysis
438	
439	Based on these findings, the following convergence opportunities exist:
440	
441	**Safe to converge (zero new state space):**
442	
443	* **Always extract and pass etag intentionally**: Every API always returns etag.
444	  Extracting it explicitly and passing it to `setIamPolicy` converts accidental
445	  passthrough or unconditional overwrites to intentionally concurrency-safe
446	  writes.  The compose function already handles etag correctly when provided.
447	  This also protects against Google-internal concurrent policy modifications
448	  that the single-writer invariant does not cover.
449	
450	* **Transient retry on SET (429/5xx)**: Currently only {rbtoe_iam_grant_project}
451	  retries transient errors.  Adding bounded retry for 429 (rate limit) and 5xx
452	  (server error) is universally correct and cannot cause new failures.
453	
454	* **Uniform etag-mismatch handling**: Handle 409 ABORTED for all standard IAM
455	  mixin APIs, and 412 Precondition Failed for {gcs_service} only.  The current
456	  project code's 412 handler should become a 409 handler.
457	
458	* **Request policy version 3 everywhere**: Protects against silent condition
459	  destruction if conditioned policies are ever introduced on repo, SA, or
460	  bucket resources.  For APIs that use GET (where version is a query param),
461	  this is a query parameter addition; for POST APIs, it is a request body
462	  field.
463	
464	**Load-bearing differences (do not homogenize):**
465	
466	* **GET vs POST for getIamPolicy**: API-mandated per service proto.
467	  Cannot be unified.
468	
469	* **PUT vs POST for setIamPolicy**: {gcs_service} requires PUT.
470	  Cannot be unified.
471	
472	* **Body wrapping** (`{"policy":...}` vs raw): {gcs_service} requires raw.
473	  Cannot be unified.
474	
475	* **Project non-200 GET is a real error**: Projects always have IAM policies.
476	  Do not vanilla-init on non-200 for projects.
477	
478	**Require investigation before converging** (tracked in <<rbshr_horizon_roadmap>>)**:**
479	
480	* **Read-back verification universalization**
481	* **SA preflight universalization**
482	* **SA numeric ID for member references**

1	==== Decision
2	
3	IAM propagation delays for Cloud Build v2 connection creation are handled by
4	connection-creation retry with exponential backoff, not by an impersonation
5	readiness gate or fixed sleep delays.
6	
7	Three propagation classes are recognized by the IAM grant harness in
8	`zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate`, all sharing the same exponential-backoff
9	budget defined by the `RBGC_PROPAGATION_*` constants:
10	
11	* **Class A — forward member-visibility**: `setIamPolicy` and `getIamPolicy`
12	  calls fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist" when a newly-created service
13	  account is not yet visible to the IAM policy service as a valid member.
14	* **Class B — backward member-visibility**: `setIamPolicy` and `getIamPolicy`
15	  calls fail with HTTP 400 "is not deleted" when a recently-deleted service
16	  account is still referenced in existing policy bindings.
17	* **Class C — caller-recently-empowered**: `getIamPolicy` / `setIamPolicy`
18	  on a resource-scope target (AR repo, SA, bucket, secret) returns HTTP 403
19	  because the caller's recently-granted role (e.g. fresh `roles/owner` on a
20	  newly-levied depot) has not yet reached the resource-scope IAM cache,
21	  even though it has reached project- and SA-scope caches.
22	
23	Class C is time-bounded only — no body fingerprinting. The bound itself is
24	the discriminator: real propagation succeeds within budget, real denial
25	waits the budget and fails cleanly. The same model is already proven in
26	{rbtgo_access_jwt_probe}. Resource-scope grant sites declare 403 tolerance
27	with an empty body-glob; project-scope sites do not.
28	
29	* **Secret access readiness**: The CB v2 connection creation LRO itself serves as the propagation test.
30	  If the Cloud Build Service Agent cannot yet access the secrets (IAM grant not propagated), the LRO fails.
31	  The caller retries with exponential backoff, deleting the zombie connection resource between attempts.
32	* **Secret resource propagation**: `getIamPolicy` on a newly created secret may return HTTP 404 while
33	  the Secret Manager resource propagates across Google's control plane.  The API documents that
34	  `getIamPolicy` returns an empty policy (HTTP 200) when no policy has been set — so 404 indicates
35	  the resource itself is not yet visible, not that the policy is absent.  After creating secrets,
36	  `rbuh_poll_until_ok` polls each secret's `getIamPolicy` endpoint until 200 before proceeding to
37	  grant IAM bindings.
38	
39	* **SA keys subresource propagation**: After
40	  link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[serviceAccounts.create]
41	  returns 200, the keys subresource read path
42	  (link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/list[serviceAccounts.keys.list])
43	  can lag the SA email path independently — empirically observed as a non-monotonic 200→200→404 flap where
44	  both the SA GET probe and a keys subresource `rbuh_poll_until_ok` probe return 200 while the real
45	  `keys.list` call immediately returns 404 on the same URL.  Mitigation: retry the actual `keys.list`
46	  call on HTTP 404 using the same backoff budget as step 7 keys.create (`RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_*`) —
47	  same underlying physics (subresource visibility after parent creation).  A separate
48	  `rbuh_poll_until_ok` proxy probe before `keys.list` was found insufficient: it returned 200 in the
49	  same flap window that the real call observed 404, exemplifying why proxy probes are not retained as
50	  correctness gates (see "What Dies" below).  See evidence row below.
51	
52	==== Rationale
53	
54	The connection creation LRO tests the actual operation, not a proxy.
55	When the Cloud Build Service Agent accesses resources requiring recently-granted permissions,
56	a successful LRO proves end-to-end readiness: the IAM grant has propagated,
57	the service agent identity is active, and the resource content is valid.
58	
59	Fixed `sleep` delays ("sleep and pray") are unreliable:
60	
61	* Google's IAM propagation documentation states changes "typically take less than 2 minutes"
62	  but provides no guaranteed upper bound.
63	* A 15-second sleep is too short for worst-case propagation and wastefully long for the common case.
64	* Retry-on-error tests the actual operation: if the grant has propagated, the retry succeeds immediately;
65	  if not, the retry continues until the deadline.
66	
67	The `testIamPermissions` API cannot serve as a readiness gate because it only tests the caller's own
68	permissions on a resource -- it cannot test whether a third-party service account (like the CB service agent)
69	has been granted access to a secret.
70	
71	==== Superseded: Impersonation Readiness Gate
72	
73	The prior approach used an impersonation readiness gate: mint a short-lived token as the CB service agent
74	via `generateAccessToken`, then call `accessSecretVersion` with that impersonated token, retrying on 403
75	until the grant propagates.  This approach was abandoned due to three problems discovered 2026-03-03:
76	
77	. **roles/owner does NOT grant iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken** --
78	  The explicit `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant (previously deleted as unnecessary per
79	  an earlier RBSCIP revision) was in fact required for impersonation.  Without it,
80	  `generateAccessToken` returns persistent 403.  roles/owner is a broad role but does not include
81	  the `iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken` permission needed for impersonation.
82	
83	. **Wrong SA identity** -- The code impersonated the default build SA
84	  (`{PN}@cloudbuild.gserviceaccount.com`) but CB v2 connections use the Cloud Build Service Agent
85	  (`service-{PN}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`).  Even if impersonation worked,
86	  it would have tested the wrong principal.
87	
88	. **No non-impersonation alternative exists** -- `testIamPermissions` and IAM Policy Analyzer
89	  reflect policy intent (what the policy document says), not enforcement state (whether the grant
90	  has actually propagated).  Only actual access as the principal proves propagation.
91	
92	Pattern B (connection-creation retry) eliminates `generateAccessToken`, `accessSecretVersion`,
93	and any need for `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grants for the readiness gate.
94

133	  this handles a different failure class (rate limiting, server errors) and is preserved inside
134	  the new outer propagation retry
135	* All IAM grant functions ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo},
136	  {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}) -- enhanced with propagation retry,

138	* Cloud Build service agent `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant on Mason SA --

141	==== Backoff Profile
142	
143	All IAM-grant propagation retries — every site in `rbgi_IAM.sh` plus the
144	inline GAR retry in `rbgg_invest_director` — use the same single profile
145	locked in `rbgc_Constants.sh`:
146	
147	* Initial delay: `RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC` (3 seconds)
148	* Multiplier: 2x (per-site arithmetic, not a kindle constant)
149	* Cap: `RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC` (20 seconds)
150	* Deadline: `RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC` (420 seconds; 7 minutes)
151	
152	This profile accommodates Google's documented "typically < 2 minutes" with margin for worst-case
153	propagation while avoiding indefinite waits. It is the single budget shared
154	across all three propagation classes (A: forward member-visibility, B:
155	backward member-visibility, C: caller-recently-empowered); operator value
156	changes propagate to every grant site by editing the constants once. The
157	JWT-bearer consumer-side retry uses its own budget
158	(`RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_*`) — different cost-of-waiting profile.

1	= Getting Started with Recipe Bottle
2	
3	{at_recipe_bottle}
4	constructs container images under controlled conditions on trusted
5	{gcp_service}
6	infrastructure.
7	Developers retrieve these
8	{at_bottle_image_s}
9	and work within identical environments.
10	The
11	{rbtgi_repository}
12	serves as the single source of truth for container artifacts.
13	
14	This guide explains the setup sequence and ongoing operational patterns.
15	Most of what follows happens once; the final sections describe day-to-day work.
16	
17	== Core Concepts
18	
19	{at_recipe_bottle} contains workloads safely.
20	A _vessel_ defines what to build--a specification for a container workload.
21	An {rbtga_ark} is the built result: the immutable artifact produced from that vessel.
22	Multiple {rbtga_ark_s} can exist for a single vessel, each with its own build timestamp and provenance.
23	
24	Each {rbtga_ark} carries strong pedigree information including SBOMs and build metadata.
25	Organizations verify these attestations before deploying.
26	
27	== Depots and Roles
28	
29	A
30	{rbtgi_depot}
31	is the logical facility where container images are constructed and retained.
32	The
33	{rbtgi_repository}
34	within the {rbtgi_depot} stores all
35	{rbtgi_image_s}
36	available for retrieval.
37	Most organizations require exactly one {rbtgi_depot}.
38	
39	{at_recipe_bottle}
40	provides bash command-line administration of {rbtgi_depot} lifecycle and operations across roles.
41	
42	* *{rbtr_payor}*: Creates and funds {rbtgi_depot_s}; creates {rbtr_governor_s}. Billing flows through the {rbtr_payor_p} linked account. Manual OAuth setup enables all subsequent automation.
43	* *{rbtr_governor}*: Administers credentials within a {rbtgi_depot}. Creates {rbtr_director_s} and {rbtr_retriever_s} via service account authentication.
44	* *{rbtr_director}*: Submits builds, manages {rbtgi_image_s}.
45	* *{rbtr_retriever}*: Pulls {rbtgi_image_s} for local use.
46	
47	The
48	{rbtr_payor}
49	stands apart--requires manual console work and OAuth authentication for both {rbtgi_depot} and {rbtr_governor} lifecycle operations.
50	The
51	{rbtr_governor}
52	and all downstream roles authenticate via
53	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
54	credentials, enabling automation without human interaction.
55	
56	== Phase 1: Establish
57	
58	{rbtgo_payor_establish}
59	is a guide for manual steps taken using the Google Cloud Console to create the
60	{rbtgi_payor_project},
61	link a billing account,
62	enable required APIs,
63	and configure an OAuth 2.0 client.
64	
65	This phase produces a
66	{rbtgi_json_key_file}
67	containing OAuth client credentials.
68	This file must not be committed to version control.
69	
70	== Phase 2: Setup
71	
72	The administrator who completed establishment now executes automation from their
73	{at_workstation}.
74	
75	{rbtgo_payor_install}
76	takes the
77	{rbtgi_json_key_file}
78	and completes the OAuth handshake.
79	A browser window opens for authorization.
80	Upon success, the
81	{rbro_refresh_token}
82	is stored in `~/.rbw/payor/rbro.env` with `600` permissions.
83	This file enables the
84	{rbtr_payor}
85	to authenticate for all future operations without repeating the browser flow.
86	
87	{rbtgo_depot_levy}
88	provisions the {at_foundry} infrastructure.
89	Most installations require exactly one
90	{rbtgi_depot}-
91	a cohesive unit comprising a dedicated
92	{gcp_project_id},
93	a
94	{rbtgi_build_bucket},
95	a
96	{rbtgi_repository},
97	and the
98	{rbtr_mason}
99	service account that executes builds within the cloud.
100	
101	The
102	{rbtr_payor}
103	can manage multiple {rbtgi_depot_s}:
104	{rbtgo_depot_list}
105	displays all active {rbtgi_depot_s} and their status;
106	{rbtgo_depot_unmake}
107	permanently removes a {rbtgi_depot} and all its resources.
108	
109	The
110	{rbtr_payor}
111	then executes
112	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
113	to create the
114	{rbtr_governor}
115	service account within the
116	{rbtgi_depot}.
117	This {rbtr_payor} operation produces an
118	{rbtgi_rbra_file},
119	stored at the path specified by
120	{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}.
121	The
122	{rbtr_governor}
123	can now operate independently of the
124	{rbtr_payor}.
125	
126	== Credential Administration
127	
128	The
129	{rbtr_governor}
130	manages the roster of credentialed users throughout the
131	{rbtgi_depot}
132	lifecycle.
133	While credential creation is typically infrequent, this administrative function remains active.
134	
135	{rbtgo_director_invest}
136	provisions a
137	{rbtr_director}
138	service account, producing an
139	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
140	at the path specified by
141	{rbrr_director_rbra_file}.
142	Directors submit builds and manage images.
143	
144	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}
145	provisions a
146	{rbtr_retriever}
147	service account, producing an
148	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
149	at the path specified by
150	{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}.
151	Retrievers pull images for local use.
152	
153	The
154	{rbtr_governor}
155	also maintains visibility into issued credentials through
156	{rbtgo_director_roster}
157	and
158	{rbtgo_retriever_roster},
159	and can revoke access through
160	{rbtgo_director_divest}
161	or
162	{rbtgo_retriever_divest}.
163	
164	== Credential Files
165	
166	Each {rbtr_role} maintains its own credential file.
167	Understanding these files helps administrators plan secure handling.
168	
169	The
170	{rbtr_payor}
171	credentials reside in `~/.rbw/payor/rbro.env`, containing the
172	{rbro_client_secret}
173	and
174	{rbro_refresh_token}.
175	This file exists only on the administrator's
176	{at_workstation}.
177	
178	The
179	{rbtr_governor},
180	{rbtr_director},
181	and
182	{rbtr_retriever}
183	each have an
184	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
185	at paths defined in the repository configuration.
186	Each file contains
187	{giam_service_account}
188	credentials scoped to one role within one
189	{rbtgi_depot}.
190	
191	All credential files require `600` permissions, exclusion from version control, and secure distribution to authorized users.
192	A credential file cannot operate outside its designated role and {rbtgi_depot}.
193	
194	== Day-to-Day Operations
195	
196	Once setup and credential administration are complete, ongoing work proceeds through
197	{rbtr_director}
198	and
199	{rbtr_retriever}
200	roles.
201	
202	=== Director Operations
203	
204	The
205	{rbtr_director}
206	manages the build lifecycle.
207	
208	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
209	submits a container build to
210	{gcb_service}
211	via `builds.create`.
212	The
213	{rbtr_mason}
214	executes the build within the cloud and publishes the resulting
215	{rbtgi_image}
216	to the
217	{rbtgi_repository}.
218	
219	{rbtgo_image_delete}
220	removes an
221	{rbtgi_image}
222	from the
223	{rbtgi_repository}.
224	
225	**MISSING**: {rbtgi_image} listing operation is not yet implemented.
226	
227	=== Retriever Operations
228	
229	The
230	{rbtr_retriever}
231	accesses built artifacts.
232	
233	{rbtgo_image_retrieve}
234	pulls an
235	{rbtgi_image}
236	from the
237	{rbtgi_repository}
238	to the local
239	{at_workstation}.
240	
241	{rbtgo_ark_inspect}
242	displays the trust posture of an
243	{rbtga_ark}
244	by querying GAR directly via the Registry API — no local summon required.
245	Authenticates as
246	{rbtr_retriever}.
247	Use {rbtc_plumb_compact} for a summary or {rbtc_plumb_full} for complete detail including package inventory and Dockerfile.
248	Hallmark-only invocation (single argument) navigates the hallmark subtree directly to locate the ark.
249	
250	== Recovery
251	
252	If `~/.rbw/payor/rbro.env` is lost, the administrator obtains a fresh
253	{rbtgi_json_key_file}
254	from Google Cloud Console and re-executes
255	{rbtgo_payor_install}.
256	
257	If OAuth tokens expire,
258	{rbtgo_payor_refresh}
259	obtains fresh credentials.
260	
261	If
262	{rbtr_governor}
263	credentials are compromised or need rotation, the
264	{rbtr_payor}
265	executes
266	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
267	to replace the existing
268	{rbtr_governor}
269	service account with a fresh one, generating new
270	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
271	credentials.
272	
273	To revoke a user's access, the
274	{rbtr_governor}
275	executes
276	{rbtgo_director_divest}
277	or
278	{rbtgo_retriever_divest}
279	for the target service account, invalidating the corresponding
280	{rbtgi_rbra_file}.
281

24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion guard
27	test -z "${ZRBTE_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
28	ZRBTE_SOURCED=1
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Kindle
32	
33	zrbte_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbte already kindled"
35	
36	  local z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
37	
38	  readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
39	  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"
40	
41	  # Suite→fixture composition is owned by theurge (RBTDRC_SUITES in
42	  # rbtdrc_crucible.rs), not bash. Suite names survive here only as tabtarget
43	  # imprints passed straight through to the binary's `suite` mode.
44	
45	  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
46	}
47	
48	######################################################################
49	# Sentinel
50	
51	zrbte_sentinel() {
52	  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
53	}
54	
55	######################################################################
56	# Internal helpers
57	
58	# zrbte_codegen() - Refresh all zipper-derived artifacts before compiling.
59	# Write-on-change, so unchanged files keep their mtime and cargo skips rebuilds.
60	# The build is the sole producer — there is no standalone generate command.
61	zrbte_codegen() {
62	  zrbte_sentinel
63	
64	  rbz_generate_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" "${RBCC_tabtarget_context_file}" \
65	    || buc_die "Failed to generate tabtarget context"
66	  rbz_generate_consts "${RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file}" \
67	    || buc_die "Failed to generate colophon consts"
68	}
69	
70	zrbte_build_binary() {
71	  zrbte_sentinel
72	
73	  zrbte_codegen
74	  buc_step "Building theurge"
75	  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
76	  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
77	}
78	
79	######################################################################
80	# Public functions
81	
82	rbte_build() {
83	  zrbte_sentinel
84	
85	  zrbte_codegen
86	  buc_step "Building theurge"
87	  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
88	  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
89	  buc_success "Theurge built"
90	}
91	
92	rbte_test() {
93	  zrbte_sentinel
94	
95	  zrbte_codegen
96	  buc_step "Testing theurge"
97	  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
98	  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
99	  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
100	}
101	
102	rbte_run() {
103	  zrbte_sentinel
104	
105	  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
106	  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture — pass one as the folio (e.g. rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh tadmor)"
107	
108	  zrbte_build_binary
109	
110	  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
111	  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${z_fixture}"
112	}
113	
114	rbte_suite() {
115	  zrbte_sentinel
116	
117	  local z_suite="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
118	  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh)"
119	
120	  zrbte_build_binary
121	
122	  # Composition is owned by theurge: pass the suite imprint straight through to
123	  # the binary's `suite` mode, which resolves and runs its fixtures.
124	  buc_step "Running theurge suite '${z_suite}'"
125	  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" suite "${z_suite}"
126	}
127	
128	rbte_single() {
129	  zrbte_sentinel
130	
131	  zrbte_build_binary
132	
133	  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
134	  local z_case="${1:-}"
135	  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single ${z_fixture:+"${z_fixture}"} ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
136	}
